3.anchor: And they supported me and did not even demand that my companion Titus be circumcised, though he was a Gentile.

4.anchor: Even that question came up only because of some so-called believers there—false ones, really

16.anchor: Yet we know that a person is made right with God by faith in Jesus Christ, not by obeying the law. And we have believed in Christ Jesus, so that we might be made right with God because of our faith in Christ, not because we have obeyed the law. For no one will ever be made right with God by obeying the law.”
16.note: Some translators hold that the quotation extends through verse 14; others through verse 16; and still others through verse 21.

20.anchor: My old self has been crucified with Christ.
20.note: Some English translations put this sentence in verse 19.

6.anchor: In the same way, “Abraham believed God, and God counted him as righteous because of his faith.”

8.anchor: What’s more, the Scriptures looked forward to this time when God would make the Gentiles right in his sight because of their faith. God proclaimed this good news to Abraham long ago when he said, “All nations will be blessed through you.”

10.anchor: But those who depend on the law to make them right with God are under his curse, for the Scriptures say, “Cursed is everyone who does not observe and obey all the commands that are written in God’s Book of the Law.”

11.anchor: So it is clear that no one can be made right with God by trying to keep the law. For the Scriptures say, “It is through faith that a righteous person has life.”

12.anchor: This way of faith is very different from the way of law, which says, “It is through obeying the law that a person has life.”

13.anchor: But Christ has rescued us from the curse pronounced by the law. When he was hung on the cross, he took upon himself the curse for our wrongdoing. For it is written in the Scriptures, “Cursed is everyone who is hung on a tree.”

14.anchor: Through Christ Jesus, God has blessed the Gentiles with the same blessing he promised to Abraham, so that we who are believers might receive the promised
14.note: Some manuscripts read the blessing of the.

16.anchor: God gave the promises to Abraham and his child.
And notice that the Scripture doesn’t say “to his children,

21.anchor: Is there a conflict, then, between God’s law and God’s promises?

27.anchor: And all who have been united with Christ in baptism have put on Christ, like putting on new clothes.

28.anchor: There is no longer Jew or Gentile,

29.anchor: And now that you belong to Christ, you are the true children

12.anchor: I just wish that those troublemakers who want to mutilate you by circumcision would mutilate themselves.
12.note: Or castrate themselves, cut themselves off from you; cut themselves off.

14.anchor: For the whole law can be summed up in this one command: “Love your neighbor as yourself.”

6.anchor: I am shocked that you are turning away so soon from God, who called you to himself through the loving mercy of Christ.
6.note: Some manuscripts read through loving mercy.

12.anchor: I received my message from no human source, and no one taught me. Instead, I received it by direct revelation from Jesus Christ.
12.note: Or by the revelation of Jesus Christ.

16.anchor: to reveal his Son to me
When this happened, I did not rush out to consult with any human being.

5.anchor: God sent him to buy freedom for us who were slaves to the law, so that he could adopt us as his very own children.

7.anchor: Now you are no longer a slave but God’s own child.

22.anchor: The Scriptures say that Abraham had two sons, one from his slave wife and one from his freeborn wife.

25.note: Greek And Hagar, which is Mount Sinai in Arabia, is now like Jerusalem; And Mount Sinai in Arabia is now like Jerusalem.
